failureStatement: Program 027 had strong specifications, design tokens, component catalogs, and explicit anti-goals. The implementation still drifted toward a generic SaaS dashboard aesthetic. The agent was compliant with expedition definitions of done but not converged with the originating human intent.
rootCause: The Mission allowed an interpretation space containing many admissible outcomes. Governance did not collapse that space to acceptable interpretations before implementation.

driftClasses.name: Generic dashboard
driftClasses.description: Metric cards, promotional banners, disconnected widgets, or other generic SaaS dashboard patterns become the dominant visual language.

driftClasses.name: Marketing-first landing
driftClasses.description: Mission Studio appears as one section among many marketing sections rather than as the homepage itself.

driftClasses.name: Chat-primary interface
driftClasses.description: Chat bubbles or decorative AI imagery become the dominant interaction model.

driftClasses.name: Page-jump navigation
driftClasses.description: Lifecycle phases are rendered as separate pages instead of a persistent workspace.

driftClasses.name: Storybook aesthetic
driftClasses.description: Components are displayed as isolated specimens rather than integrated into a coherent workspace.

driftClasses.name: Placeholder artifacts
driftClasses.description: Fake terminal output, mock data, or other placeholders unrelated to runtime are used.

driftClasses.name: Hardcoded values
driftClasses.description: Visual values are defined outside the LDS-002 token system.

driftClasses.name: Workspace dilution
driftClasses.description: Mission Studio shell is present but not the dominant visual artifact.

validBranches.name: Persistent workspace
validBranches.description: Mission Studio is the homepage; lifecycle phases are scroll-driven workspace states.

validBranches.name: Hero invitation
validBranches.description: A short hero section invites the visitor into Mission Studio, then transitions into the persistent workspace.

validBranches.name: Deterministic demo
validBranches.description: A simulated operator execution demonstrates SYNTH deterministically without requiring a live agent.

validBranches.name: Light-theme default
validBranches.description: The workspace defaults to a light theme; dark mode is optional.

correctiveActions.purpose: Intent capture, refinement, alignment contract, divergence gate
Review gate, acceptance gate, execution-correctness enforcement
